label: Typeform
description: Connect to Typeform to read forms, fetch responses, and manage surveys.

form.title: Connect Typeform
form.subtitle: Paste your Typeform personal access token to let Anton read your forms and responses.

methods.label: Personal Access Token
methods.description: Paste a personal access token (starts with tfp_…) generated from your Typeform account settings. The fastest way to connect — no OAuth app setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Typeform Personal Access Token

Typeform personal access tokens give Anton scoped read access to your forms and responses without requiring a full OAuth app.

### Steps

1. Log in to [Typeform](https://www.typeform.com) and click your profile avatar in the top-right corner.
2. Select **Settings** from the dropdown menu.
3. In the left sidebar, click **Personal tokens**.
4. Click **Generate a new token**.
5. Give the token a name like `Anton` so you can identify it later.
6. Select the scopes Anton needs — at minimum enable **Read** access for **Forms** and **Responses**.
7. Click **Generate token**. The token is shown once — copy it immediately.
8. Paste the token (starts with `tfp_…`) into the **Personal access token** field below.

### Notes

- Tokens are shown only once. If you lose it, delete the old token and generate a new one.
- To revoke access, go back to **Settings → Personal tokens** and click the trash icon next to the token.
- Workspace tokens (for team accounts) can be created under **Workspace settings → Tokens** instead.

fields.name: access_token
fields.label: Personal access token
fields.description: Your Typeform personal access token. Found under Settings → Personal tokens. Shown once on creation — copy it before closing the dialog.

fields.name: workspace_id
fields.label: Workspace ID (optional)
fields.description: Optional. Scope requests to a specific Typeform workspace. Leave blank to use your default account workspace.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (bring your own app)
methods.description: Use a Typeform OAuth app you've registered in the Typeform Developer Portal. More complex to set up but useful for multi-user or organization-wide deployments.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 with a Typeform App

This method requires registering an app in the Typeform Developer Portal and providing the client credentials below.

### Steps

1. Go to the [Typeform Developer Portal](https://developer.typeform.com/) and sign in.
2. Navigate to **My Apps → Create a new app**.
3. Fill in the app name (e.g., `Anton`) and set the **Redirect URI** to `http://127.0.0.1` (Anton uses a local redirect).
4. Under **Scopes**, enable: `forms:read`, `responses:read`, `workspaces:read`, `accounts:read`.
5. Save the app. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** shown on the app detail page.
6. Paste both values into the fields below, then click **Submit** to launch the OAuth consent flow in your browser.

### Notes

- Client secrets are sensitive — treat them like passwords.
- If you need to revoke access, delete or reset the app in the Developer Portal.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth client ID
fields.description: From the Typeform Developer Portal → My Apps → your app → Client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth client secret
fields.description: Paired with the client ID. Found on the same app detail page in the Developer Portal.
